FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Component

Namespace
FluentGpu.Hooks
Assembly
FluentGpu.Hooks.dll

Base for stateful components. Override Render(); use hooks in stable order each render.

public abstract class Component

MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?)

Properties

Context 

public RenderContext Context { get; }

Property Value

RenderContext

RunsOnce

True for run-once (signals-native) components: the reconciler runs their body untracked, so the render-effect never re-subscribes and never re-renders — reactivity comes purely from the bindings/For/Show inside. False for classic Components, whose render-effect re-runs on their own state/context changes (granular).

public virtual bool RunsOnce { get; }

Property Value

bool

Methods

PrefetchImage(string, int)

Prefetch an image the UI is about to need so it's resident before it scrolls in.

protected void PrefetchImage(string src, int decodePx)

src string
decodePx int

Render() 

public abstract Element Render()

Returns

Element

RenderWithHooks()

Run one render pass with hook bookkeeping.

Returns

Element

Unmount()

Called by the reconciler when this component leaves the tree — run effect cleanups.

UseFloatSignal(float)

A persistent scalar signal for the hot path; bind it to a node channel for a render-free update.

protected FloatSignal UseFloatSignal(float initial = 0)

Parameters

initial float

Returns

FloatSignal

UseImage(string, int, ImagePriority, string?)

Bind an async image and observe its load state (spinner / error fallback). Pair with Ui.Image to paint it.

protected ImageBinding UseImage(string src, int decodePx, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible, string? blurHash = null)

decodePx int
priority ImagePriority
blurHash string

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
